scope: FQ-09W: give the side-view world scenic depth (backdrop plane), make mining reveal cave walls instead of the clear viewport (deterministic natural backing walls), and stop day-white ambient from following the player underground (depth-aware cave darkness) - without touching collision, mining, shelter/light math, or the save format.
summary: Three separate visual planes are now live and provably isolated. (1) Scenic backdrop: scripts/world/world_backdrop.gd at z -10 inside the modulated canvas - code-drawn sky gradient bands reaching the DEEPEST valley line (so varying terrain never exposes a void), far/mid silhouette ridges with 2px-stepped parallax stable in world space, light_mask 0 so torches cannot splash glow onto distant scenery, optional backgrounds/ image hooks (sky full-frame, far/mid tiling strips). (2) Natural backing walls: a BackgroundWalls TileMapLayer at z -2, rebuilt each world.setup from the PRISTINE generated surface + generation.dirt_depth (dirt-wall band then stone wall, nothing at/above the surface row) - deterministic from seed/config by construction, never in cells/deltas/saves, and its tileset carries zero physics and zero occlusion layers so walls are structurally unable to affect collision, lighting, shelter, or settlement math; tiles from back_walls/ art when present else the matching block texture darkened and fully opaque. (3) Underground darkness: world.sky_line(x) = first solid cell of the LIVE column (cached, invalidated per column on any block change) feeds game_root.ambient_darkness_factor() / ambient_target_color(), pushing the day/night/storm base toward CAVE_TINT over a 6-cell fade band, consumed by both the per-frame tint lerp and the instant load path - mining an open shaft re-admits real daylight to its floor while a sealed column at the same depth stays dark. The directional-shadow sunlight path was assessed against scout evidence (occluders are tileset-level, blocks_light-only; no DirectionalLight2D exists) and recorded as the future path per the work order's fallback clause; the shipped model is documented as a first-slice approximation with no lateral bleed.
acceptance: Same seed/config -> identical wall map, dirt band above stone, nothing at/above the surface row, wall tileset 0 physics / 0 occlusion layers (fq09w_walls_deterministic_and_inert)
A mined below-surface block stays a normal air delta while wall_at still reports the wall behind it (fq09w_mined_chamber_reveals_wall)
Midday underground ambient target reaches the cave tint while the surface stays exactly DAY_TINT - asserted on ambient_target_color, not the smoothing lerp (fq09w_underground_dark_at_midday)
A freshly mined open shaft admits daylight to its floor; a sealed column at the same depth stays dark (fq09w_open_shaft_admits_daylight)
Backdrop behind walls behind blocks, image hooks null -> code fallback (fq09w_backdrop_behind_world); back_walls art resolves and falls back with self-cleaning temp art (fq09w_wall_art_hook)
Saved world restores cleanly; no new save keys; all legacy save/load, torch, occlusion, night/storm checks green (fq09w_world_restored; suite 210/210)
Screenshot tour frame review: day settlement with backdrop and no lighting artifacts, night torchlight, new 09_underground_midday_torch chamber shot
validation: python scripts/validate_repo.py -> PASS incl. world_backdrop.gd, backgrounds/back_walls dirs and categories
COHERONIA_SMOKE=1 waited windowed run -> 210/210 PASS at 2026-07-10T11:47:17

haiku read-only diff verifier -> NO FINDINGS (wall inertness, sky-line invalidation coverage, backdrop loop/division guards, ambient writer completeness, 7 checks counted, tour save-safety, validator additions, doc claims)
decisions: Column-skylight ambient over directional-shadow sunlight this slice: scout evidence showed occluders are tileset-level and only on blocks_light blocks with no DirectionalLight2D anywhere, so real shadow-cast sunlight needs an occluder redesign - the work order's sanctioned fallback applies and the approximation is documented, test-hooked (ambient_darkness_factor/sky_line), and honest about its limits
Walls derive from the PRISTINE generated surface, not post-delta state: player edits can never alter the wall map, and determinism is by construction rather than by storage
Wall inertness is structural: the wall tileset simply has no physics or occlusion layers to configure, so no future wall variant can accidentally gain collision or light-blocking
The backdrop lives inside the modulated world canvas (not a CanvasLayer) so day/night/storm tint applies to it for free; light_mask 0 keeps local lights off distant scenery
Sky fill extends to the deepest valley line - found by frame review when the average-horizon fill left a black void behind low terrain
Swarm: 3 parallel haiku read-only scouts (rendering/lighting, worldgen/saves, conventions/isolation), one implementation lead owning all edits, 1 haiku read-only diff verifier
risks: Ambient follows the player's column: the whole canvas darkens when buried (surface visible at screen edges darkens too) - first-slice approximation; per-cell skylight is the recorded future path
Backdrop ridges are cosmetic and do not correspond to real terrain (template-compliant: below foreground contrast, non-colliding)
Only dirt/stone wall materials ship; ore/fungal/crystal/timber walls and cave/deep background layers are FQ-09A roadmap items
Constructed player-placeable walls (drops, wall deltas, recipes, save migration) remain a deliberately separate future gameplay task
Repo still ships zero art; both new hook categories are proven via temp files
next_action: FQ-09A: future asset manifest and prompt packs (opening cel frames, background layers, back-wall tiles included).
